{"key_event":"Taif settlement and Syrian-backed recentralization","reasoning":"The end of the civil war, militia demobilization, and rebuilding of the army, police, ministries, courts, and taxation lifted Lebanon from near-collapse to a moderate but still uneven state, constrained by Syrian tutelage, Hezbollah’s arms, Israeli occupation, and autonomous camps. Civil society remained organized through parties, media, NGOs, sectarian networks, and Hezbollah’s welfare institutions, but Syrian-managed politics, repression of opposition, patronage, impunity, and displacement reduced its autonomous ability to check power.","society_change":-1.3,"society_power":5.5,"state_change":3.4,"state_power":5.2}
